rulings.Case Title: SHERRI WATSON VS. ROBERT G GISH M.D.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Ptlf'S Responses To Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, July 17, 2013, Line 1: Defendant CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Responses to Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, no opposition. Plaintiff is to provide verified, objection-free responses within 10 days of receiving notice of the order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FORD MOTOR CREDIT COMPANY VS. ROGER CARDENAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File X-Complt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 17, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT ROGER CARDENAS' MOTION FOR LEAVE To File Cross-Complaint. Denied, without prejudice. Defendant's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint and leave to amend answer is procedurally deficient. As to the leave sought to file a cross-complaint, Defendant has not provided any declaration to show that it would be in the interest of justice to grant leave to file; and some reasonable excuse why the cross-complaint was not filed earlier. (CCP § 426.50.) As to the leave sought to amend an answer, Defendant has not complied with CRC 3.1324(a) and (b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON & REES LLP VS. STEVEN ANTHONY LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Motion To Compel Furtehr Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One) And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defts Steven Lopez And Eric Koehler
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, July 17, 2013, Line 4: Plaintiff GORDON & REES LLP'S Motion To Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories (Set One) and for Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants Steven Lopez and Eric Koehler Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance required. Parties are to meet and confer further before the hearing on July 17 and attempt to resolve issues as to those discovery requests that are still outstanding, and be prepared at the hearing to advise the Court of precisely which discovery requests are still at issue. By way of guidance: 1) the Court will not sustain objections to form interrogatories; 2) requests for more time to respond to interrogatories are not appropriate given the age of this dispute; 3) responses must be particularized to the interrogatory asked-- no "shotgun" responses. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Palo Alto Medical Foundation, Gregory Engle, M.D. Dr. Conrad Massimo Vial And Dr. Graham Beaumont And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, July 17, 2013, Line 5: Plaintiff FRANCES SUSSNA'S Motion to Quash Deposition Subpoena for Production of Business Records to Palo Alto Medical Foundation, Gregory Engle, M.D., Dr. Conrad Massimo Vial, and Dr. Graham Beaumont and for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance required. Parties are to meet and confer further before the hearing on July 17 and attempt to resolve issues concerning the scope of the subpoenas. By way of guidance, the Court intends to limit the scope of the subpoenas to the areas of the body (apparently only cardiac) to which injury is claimed under Britt and its progeny. With regard to mental health records, assuming plaintiff is placing mental condition in issue, the Court intends to limit discovery from her therapist to those records that refer or relate to the mental injury claimed. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: YAN KALIKA et al VS. MICHAEL SCHIMMEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Michael Schimmel
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, July 17, 2013, Line 6: Plaintiffs YAN KALIKA'S and AA CONDOS LLC'S Motion to Compel Attendance and Testimony of Michael Schimmel Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Michael Schimmel and Eric Roe are again ordered to appear for deposition, for purposes of this Order, by August 1, 2013. The Court finds that Schimmel and Roe have violated prior court orders. As they have already been sanctioned monetarily and found in contempt, the next level of sanction is to strike all affirmative defenses stated in the defendants' Answer. The Court orders all affirmative defenses stated in the defendants' Answer stricken. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance required. Parties are to meet and confer further before the hearing on July 17 and attempt to resolve issue concerning the scope of the subpoena pursuant to Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844 and its progeny. The Court has considered the Opposition brief. By way of guidance, the Court intends to limit the scope of the subpoenas to those records that refer or relate to the mental injury claimed. =(302/JPT)
